title: Detection of Machine Learning Model Attacks Obfuscated in Unicode
summary: A prompt for a generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) model which contains unicode is received. The prompt is then tokenized to result in a plurality of tokens. Token forming part of a repeating sequence are identified and then removed to result in a modified set of tokens. The modified set of tokens are subsequently detokenized to result in a modified prompt. It is then determined, whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI model will result in the GenAI model behaving in an undesired manner. The modified prompt is passed to the GenAI model when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt will not result in the GenAI model behaving in an undesired manner. Otherwise, at least one remediation action is initiated when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI model will result in the GenAI model behaving in an undesired manner.
details:
The following listing of claims replaces all prior listings of claims:
1
1. (Original) A computer implemented method comprising:
receiving, by a proxy executing in a computing environment of a monitored generative artificial intelligence (GenAI
) model, a prompt for the GenAI
 model comprising unicode;
tokenizing the prompt to result in a plurality of tokens;
identifying and removing tokens forming part of a repeating sequence to result in a modified set of tokens;
detokenizing the modified set of tokens to result in a modified prompt;
determining whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner;
passing the modified prompt to the GenAI
 model when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt will not result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner; and
initiating at least one remediation action when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner.
Original

2
2. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the removed tokens all have a same corresponding value.
Original

3
3. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the removed tokens all have values within a predefined range.
Original

4
4. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the determination of whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner comprises:
comparing at least a portion of the modified prompt to a dictionary of strings known to cause the GenAI
 model to behave in an undesired manner.

Original

5
5. (Original) The method of claim 4, 
wherein the comparing identifies matching entries in the dictionary.
Original

6
6. (Original) The method of claim 4, 
wherein the comparing uses distance measurements for the at least a portion of the modified prompt relative to the strings in the dictionary.
Original

7
7. (Original) The method of claim 6, 
wherein the distance measurements are based on a Levenshtein distance.
Original

8
8. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the determination of whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner is based on a perplexity measurement of the modified prompt.
Original

9
9. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the GenAI
 model comprises a large language model.
Original

10
10. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the at least one remediation action prevents the prompt from being input into the GenAI
 model.
Original

11
11. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the at least one remediation action flags the prompt as being malicious for quality assurance.
Original

12
12. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the at least one remediation action sanitizes the prompt to be benign and causes the sanitized prompt to be ingested by the GenAI
 model.
Original

13
13. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the at least one remediation action blocks an internet protocol (IP) address of a requester of the prompt.

Original

14
14. (Original) The method of claim 1, 
wherein the at least one remediation action causes subsequent prompts from an entity identified by one or more of an internet protocol (IP) address, a media access control (MAC) address, or a session identifier of a requester of the prompt to be further modified upon a determination and causes such further modified prompt to be ingested by the GenAI
 model.
Original

15
15. (Original) computer implemented method comprising:
receiving, by an analysis engine, a prompt for ingestion by a generative artificial intelligence (GenAI
) model comprising unicode;
tokenizing, by the analysis engine, the prompt to result in a plurality of tokens;
identifying and removing, by the analysis engine, tokens forming part of a repeating sequence to result in a modified set of tokens;
detokenizing the modified set of tokens to result in a modified prompt;
determining, by the analysis engine, whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner;
passing, by the analysis engine, the modified prompt to the GenAI
 model when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt will not result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner; and
initiating, by a remediation engine, at least one remediation action when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner.
Original

16
16. (Original) The method of claim 15, 
wherein the removed tokens all have a same corresponding value.
Original

17
17. (Original) The method of claim 15, 
wherein the removed tokens all have values within a predefined range.
Original

18
18. (Original) The method of claim 15, 
wherein the determination of whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner comprises:

comparing at least a portion of the modified prompt to a dictionary of strings known to cause the GenAI
 model to behave in an undesired manner.
Original

19
19. (Original) The method of claim 18, 
wherein the comparing identifies matching entries in the dictionary.
Original

20
20. (Original) The method of claim 18, 
wherein the comparing uses distance measurements for the at least a portion of the modified prompt relative to the strings in the dictionary.
Original

21
21. (Original) The method of claim 20, 
wherein the distance measurements are based on a Levenshtein distance.
Original

22
22. (Original) The method of claim 15, 
wherein the determination of whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner is based on a perplexity measurement of the modified prompt.
Original

23
23. (Currently Amended) A system comprising:
a model computing environment comprising one or more computing devices executing a generative artificial intelligence (GenAI
) model and a proxy, each computing device of the model computing environment comprising at least one hardware processor and memory;
a monitoring computing environment, separate and distinct from the model computing environment, comprising one or more computing devices executing an analysis engine and a remediation engine, each computing device of the monitoring computing environment comprising at least one hardware processor and memory;
wherein;the proxy receives a prompt for the GenAI
 model comprising unicode;
the prompt is tokenized[[ing]] the prompt to result in a plurality of tokens;
the analysis engine identifies and removes tokens forming part of a repeating sequence to result in a modified set of tokens;

the analysis engine detokenizes the modified set of tokens to result in a modified prompt;
the analysis engine determines whether ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner;
the analysis engine passes the modified prompt to the GenAI
 model when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt will not result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner; and
the remediation engine initiates at least one remediation action when it is determined that ingestion of the modified prompt by the GenAI
 model will result in the GenAI
 model behaving in an undesired manner.

Currently amended
